creator: robert.burmeister@utoledo.edu
text:
Build on FreeBSD i386 9.1RC3 results in build error:
./../include/net-server.h:32:19: error: verto.h: No such file or directory

http://svnweb.freebsd.org/ports/head/security/krb5/pkg-plist?r1=306935&r2=306934&pathrev=306935

added
include/verto.h
on November 3rd.

Fix: 

revert to r306934 ?

note:
http://web.mit.edu/kerberos/krb5-1.10/krb5-1.10/doc/CHANGES

r25170 | ghudson | 2011-09-09 17:02:55 -0400 (Fri, 09 Sep 2011) | 5 lines
Changed paths:
   M /trunk/src/config/pre.in
   M /trunk/src/kadmin/server/deps
   M /trunk/src/kdc/deps
   M /trunk/src/lib/apputils/deps
   M /trunk/src/util/depfix.pl
   M /trunk/src/util/k5ev/deps
   M /trunk/src/util/verto/deps

Fix verto.h dependencies for system libverto.

When we have conditionally built bundled source, we need logic in
depfix.pl and variables in pre.in to avoid depending on the bundled
verto.h in generated dependencies.  Add that logic for verto.
How-To-Repeat: Use FreeBSD i386 9
portsnap a fresh ports tree
cd usr/ports/security/krb5/
make distclean
make

results in:
Stop in /usr/ports/security/krb5.
*** [build] Error code 1

(note: many dependancies on krb5)
